text:
Can we please stop with the "This is how you make trailers", "This trailer revealed too much", and "This trailer gave everything away" comments? It's so annoying. Trailers should reveal a snapshot of the story. They can't give everything away because it's just a trailer. Even then, watching the movie is also about the journey, the emotions and characters and such. Otherwise, you could just stick with the Wikipedia summary, if all you care about is what happens.

That being said, I'm not complaining about this trailer. I do think this trailer was done well. It wasn't just a bunch of out of chaotic scenes with no plot details. There was a plot revealed, but we weren't told why what was happening was happening. I think that works well because the characters don't know why that's all happening either. So if we were to watch the movie, we'd get to figure it out along with the characters.

text: My wife and I managed to snag tickets to Weapons at the very last second, and we literally rushed to make it to the theater on time. We’re so glad we did, because this turned out to be one of the best movie experiences we’ve had in years. From start to finish, the film kept us completely locked in, smart, intense, and so well put together. At one point my wife screamed like a girl while I couldn’t help but laugh at the scary parts, which made the whole experience even better. I also realized my eyes haven’t been that wide open in years. The sheer intensity of the movie kept me glued to the screen. We walked out of the theater saying the same thing. Weapons isn’t just good, it’s SiCK, twisted and truly unforgettable.

text: I didn’t have high expectations for the movie since I’ve been disappointed by far too many horror movies that were heavily marketed (ehem Longlegs although a lot of people like it) but this was the best movie I’ve watched so this year! It just kept getting better and better as the movie progressed! The concept isn’t the most original but the story was so refreshingly unique and the execution was absolutely masterful. Every single actor chosen was perfect for their roles.  As a horror fan I can’t wait to see more movies from this studio in the future!
